Today, the dance is performed according to the Gregorian calendar. On the 14th, it is first offered at each settlement's {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}}, followed by performances at requested households.
The performers include: * '''Kuwakashira''' (鍬頭): Dressed in {{ill|naga-juban|ja|長襦袢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/naga-juban|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 襦袢|1=Zhongyi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55358825}} and tied with {{ill|donko|ja|緞子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/donko|1=donko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11607976}}, wearing a twisted {{ill|Tenugui|en|Tenugui|WD=Q2403941|lt=tenugui}} headband and carrying a large gourd. * '''Tane-oroshi''' (種下ろし): Two singers dressed in {{ill|formal kimono|ja|紋付羽織袴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/formal kimono|1=montsuki-haori-hakama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605315}}, armed with a {{ill|Wakizashi|en|Wakizashi|WD=Q221693|lt=wakizashi}} and holding {{ill|Hand fan|en|Hand fan|WD=Q193220|lt=sensu}}. * '''Taiko-uchi''' (太鼓打ち): Two drummers wearing {{ill|formal hakama|ja|袷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/formal hakama|1=awase|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11626642}} tied with a {{ill|yaku-obi|ja|奴帯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yaku-obi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with rolled-up kimono hems, white {{ill|mohimo|ja|股引|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mohimo|1=momohiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611516}}, and {{ill|Kyahan|en|Kyahan|WD=Q6403679|lt=Kyahan}}, carrying {{ill|Drum|en|Drum|WD=Q11404|lt=taiko}} and sticks. * '''Sasara-zuri''' (ささら摺り): Two boys aged 14–15 wearing {{ill|Kasuri|en|Kasuri|WD=Q3081545|lt=kasuri}} garments, equipped with {{ill|Binzasara|en|Binzasara|WD=Q2904003|lt=Binzasara}} (wooden clappers). * '''Saotome''' (早乙女): Several adult male performers in {{ill|Edo-tsuma|ja|江戸褄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo-tsuma|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} kimono, wearing {{ill|Okoso zukin|ja|おこそ頭巾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okoso zukin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} hoods and holding {{ill|yotsutake|ja|四つ竹|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yotsutake|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or sensu.
The performance begins with a speech by the Kuwakashira, followed by dance sequences symbolizing "Seed Sowing," "Rice Planting," "Harvesting," "Threshing," "Milling," "Winnowing," and "Ending Ceremony." The ritual concludes with the Kuwakashira delivering congratulatory remarks.
== References == * "Matsuri, Geinō, Gyōji Daijiten (Vol. 2)" (朝倉書店, 2009), ISBN 978-4-254-50013-4.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:津島の田植踊]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:津島の田植踊]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:津島の田植踊]]
'''Tsushima no Taue Odori''' (津島の田植踊) is a traditional folk performance of rice planting dance from {{ill|Tsunami, Namie, Fukushima|ja|福島県双葉郡浪江町津島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunami, Namie, Fukushima|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Japan. It is recognized as a {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation|lt=Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|選択無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 記録作成等の措置を講ずべき無形の民俗文化財}} by the national government and as a prefectural {{ill|Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} of Fukushima Prefecture.
This rice planting dance is a form of {{ill|Yoshuku|lt=Yoshuku|lt=Yoshuku performing arts|ja|予祝芸能|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshuku performing arts|qq=}} that prays for abundant harvests, passed down through four villages in Namie: Kamitsushima, Shimotsushima, Minamitsushima, and Akugun. It was traditionally performed on {{ill|January 14th (lunar calendar)|ja|1月14日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 14th (lunar calendar)|1=14th day of the 1st month in the Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q839082}}, {{ill|January 15th (lunar calendar)|ja|1月15日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 15th (lunar calendar)|1=15th day of the 1st month in the Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q838395}}, and {{ill|January 17th (lunar calendar)|ja|1月17日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 17th (lunar calendar)|1=17th day of the 1st month in the Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q838983}} but is now conducted according to the Gregorian calendar. On the first day, January 14th, the dance is dedicated at each village's respective {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shrine}}, followed by performances at houses upon request.
The performance features distinct roles: * '''Kuwagashira''' (鍬頭): The leader who wears a {{ill|Nagajuban|ja|長襦袢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagajuban|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 襦袢|1=Zhongyi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55358825}}, secures a brocade sash (緞子), twists a {{ill|Tenugui|en|Tenugui|WD=Q2403941|lt=Tenugui}} around their head, and carries a large calabash. * '''Taneoroshi''' (種下ろし): Two performers in {{ill|Mon-tsuki haori hakama|ja|紋付羽織袴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mon-tsuki haori hakama|1=montsuki-haori-hakama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605315}}, equi11ed with short swords and folding fans, who sing. * '''Taiko-uchi''' (太鼓打ち): Two performers in mon-tsuki, tied with a {{ill|Yaku-himo|ja|奴帯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaku-himo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who wear white {{ill|Matabiki|ja|股引|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matabiki|1=momohiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611516}} and gaiters, playing drums with sticks. * '''Sasara-suri''' (ささら摺り): Two performers in {{ill|Kasuri|en|Kasuri|WD=Q3081545|lt=Kasuri}} kimono, wearing short pants, and holding bamboo cla11ers (sasara). These roles are played by boys aged 14–15, while all other roles are performed by adult men. * '''Saotome''' (早乙女): Several performers wearing Edo-style tsumugi kimono and {{ill|Okoso zukin|ja|おこそ頭巾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okoso zukin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} headdresses, carrying small fans and four-legged bamboo sticks.
The dance begins with the Kuwagashira's prologue and progresses through themes such as "Seed Planting," "Rice Planting," "Harvesting," "Threshing," "Hulling," "Using the Winnowing Machine," and "Ritual Completion tier 3]] {{Japan-culture-stub}}
}}

==Merged second translation== '''Tsushima no Taue Odori''' (津島の田植踊) is a {{ill|rice-planting dance|ja|田植踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice-planting dance|qq=}}, a form of folk performing art, passed down in {{ill|Tsunami, Namie|ja|浪江町津島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunami, Namie|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Namie, Fukushima|en|Namie, Fukushima|WD=Q1023937|lt=Namie, Fukushima}}, located in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|WD=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture}}, Japan. It has been designated as a {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation|lt=Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|選択無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 記録作成等の措置を講ずべき無形の民俗文化財}} by the national government and as a {{ill|Prefectural Cultural Property of Japan|ja|都道府県指定無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Cultural Property of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} by Fukushima Prefecture.
This rice-planting dance is a {{ill|Yoshuku|lt=Yoshuku|lt=Yoshuku-geino|ja|予祝芸能|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yoshuku-geino|qq=}} (ritual performance for blessings of bountiful harvests) originating from four {{ill|Human settlement|en|Human settlement|WD=Q486972|lt=settlements}} in Tsushima: Kamitsushima, Shimotsushima, Minamitsushima, and Akaogi. It was traditionally performed on the {{ill|14th day of the 1st lunar month|ja|1月14日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/14th day of the 1st lunar month|qq=}}, {{ill|15th day of the 1st lunar month|ja|1月15日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/15th day of the 1st lunar month|qq=}}, and {{ill|17th day of the 1st lunar month|ja|1月17日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/17th day of the 1st lunar month|qq=}}. Today, the dance is performed according to the Gregorian calendar. On the 14th, it is first offered at each settlement's {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}}, followed by performances at requested households.
The performers include: * '''Kuwakashira''' (鍬頭): Dressed in {{ill|naga-juban|ja|長襦袢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/naga-juban|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 襦袢|1=Zhongyi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55358825}} and tied with {{ill|donko|ja|緞子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/donko|1=donko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11607976}}, wearing a twisted {{ill|Tenugui|en|Tenugui|WD=Q2403941|lt=tenugui}} headband and carrying a large gourd. * '''Tane-oroshi''' (種下ろし): Two singers dressed in {{ill|formal kimono|ja|紋付羽織袴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/formal kimono|1=montsuki-haori-hakama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605315}}, armed with a {{ill|Wakizashi|en|Wakizashi|WD=Q221693|lt=wakizashi}} and holding {{ill|Hand fan|en|Hand fan|WD=Q193220|lt=sensu}}. * '''Taiko-uchi''' (太鼓打ち): Two drummers wearing {{ill|formal hakama|ja|袷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/formal hakama|1=awase|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11626642}} tied with a {{ill|yaku-obi|ja|奴帯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yaku-obi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with rolled-up kimono hems, white {{ill|mohimo|ja|股引|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mohimo|1=momohiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611516}}, and {{ill|Kyahan|en|Kyahan|WD=Q6403679|lt=Kyahan}}, carrying {{ill|Drum|en|Drum|WD=Q11404|lt=taiko}} and sticks. * '''Sasara-zuri''' (ささら摺り): Two boys aged 14–15 wearing {{ill|Kasuri|en|Kasuri|WD=Q3081545|lt=kasuri}} garments, equipped with {{ill|Binzasara|en|Binzasara|WD=Q2904003|lt=Binzasara}} (wooden clappers). * '''Saotome''' (早乙女): Several adult male performers in {{ill|Edo-tsuma|ja|江戸褄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo-tsuma|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} kimono, wearing {{ill|Okoso zukin|ja|おこそ頭巾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okoso zukin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} hoods and holding {{ill|yotsutake|ja|四つ竹|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yotsutake|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or sensu.
The performance begins with a speech by the Kuwakashira, followed by dance sequences symbolizing "Seed Sowing," "Rice Planting," "Harvesting," "Threshing," "Milling," "Winnowing," and "Ending Ceremony." The ritual concludes with the Kuwakashira delivering congratulatory remarks.

Today, the dance is performed according to the Gregorian calendar. On the 14th, it is first offered at each settlement's {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}}, followed by performances at requested households.
The performers include: * '''Kuwakashira''' (鍬頭): Dressed in {{ill|naga-juban|ja|長襦袢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/naga-juban|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 襦袢|1=Zhongyi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55358825}} and tied with {{ill|donko|ja|緞子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/donko|1=donko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11607976}}, wearing a twisted {{ill|Tenugui|en|Tenugui|WD=Q2403941|lt=tenugui}} headband and carrying a large gourd. * '''Tane-oroshi''' (種下ろし): Two singers dressed in {{ill|formal kimono|ja|紋付羽織袴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/formal kimono|1=montsuki-haori-hakama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605315}}, armed with a {{ill|Wakizashi|en|Wakizashi|WD=Q221693|lt=wakizashi}} and holding {{ill|Hand fan|en|Hand fan|WD=Q193220|lt=sensu}}. * '''Taiko-uchi''' (太鼓打ち): Two drummers wearing {{ill|formal hakama|ja|袷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/formal hakama|1=awase|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11626642}} tied with a {{ill|yaku-obi|ja|奴帯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yaku-obi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with rolled-up kimono hems, white {{ill|mohimo|ja|股引|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mohimo|1=momohiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611516}}, and {{ill|Kyahan|en|Kyahan|WD=Q6403679|lt=Kyahan}}, carrying {{ill|Drum|en|Drum|WD=Q11404|lt=taiko}} and sticks. * '''Sasara-zuri''' (ささら摺り): Two boys aged 14–15 wearing {{ill|Kasuri|en|Kasuri|WD=Q3081545|lt=kasuri}} garments, equipped with {{ill|Binzasara|en|Binzasara|WD=Q2904003|lt=Binzasara}} (wooden clappers). * '''Saotome''' (早乙女): Several adult male performers in {{ill|Edo-tsuma|ja|江戸褄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo-tsuma|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} kimono, wearing {{ill|Okoso zukin|ja|おこそ頭巾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okoso zukin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} hoods and holding {{ill|yotsutake|ja|四つ竹|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yotsutake|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or sensu.
The performance begins with a speech by the Kuwakashira, followed by dance sequences symbolizing "Seed Sowing," "Rice Planting," "Harvesting," "Threshing," "Milling," "Winnowing," and "Ending Ceremony." The ritual concludes with the Kuwakashira delivering congratulatory remarks.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:津島の田植踊]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:津島の田植踊]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:津島の田植踊]]
'''Tsushima no Taue Odori''' (津島の田植踊) is a traditional folk performance of rice planting dance from {{ill|Tsunami, Namie, Fukushima|ja|福島県双葉郡浪江町津島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunami, Namie, Fukushima|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Japan. It is recognized as a {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation|lt=Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|選択無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 記録作成等の措置を講ずべき無形の民俗文化財}} by the national government and as a prefectural {{ill|Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} of Fukushima Prefecture.
This rice planting dance is a form of {{ill|Yoshuku|lt=Yoshuku|lt=Yoshuku performing arts|ja|予祝芸能|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshuku performing arts|qq=}} that prays for abundant harvests, passed down through four villages in Namie: Kamitsushima, Shimotsushima, Minamitsushima, and Akugun. It was traditionally performed on {{ill|January 14th (lunar calendar)|ja|1月14日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 14th (lunar calendar)|1=January 14 (Lunisolar)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q839082}}, {{ill|January 15th (lunar calendar)|ja|1月15日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 15th (lunar calendar)|1=January 15 (Lunisolar)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q838395}}, and {{ill|January 17th (lunar calendar)|ja|1月17日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 17th (lunar calendar)|1=January 17 (Lunisolar)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q838983}} but is now conducted according to the Gregorian calendar. On the first day, January 14th, the dance is dedicated at each village's respective {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shrine|1=Shinto shrine}}, followed by performances at houses upon request.
The performance features distinct roles: * '''Kuwagashira''' (鍬頭): The leader who wears a {{ill|Nagajuban|ja|長襦袢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagajuban|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 襦袢|1=Zhongyi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55358825}}, secures a brocade sash (緞子), twists a {{ill|Tenugui|en|Tenugui|WD=Q2403941|lt=Tenugui|1=Tenugui}} around their head, and carries a large calabash. * '''Taneoroshi''' (種下ろし): Two performers in {{ill|Mon-tsuki haori hakama|ja|紋付羽織袴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mon-tsuki haori hakama|1=montsuki-haori-hakama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605315}}, equi11ed with short swords and folding fans, who sing. * '''Taiko-uchi''' (太鼓打ち): Two performers in mon-tsuki, tied with a {{ill|Yaku-himo|ja|奴帯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaku-himo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who wear white {{ill|Matabiki|ja|股引|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matabiki|1=momohiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611516}} and gaiters, playing drums with sticks. * '''Sasara-suri''' (ささら摺り): Two performers in {{ill|Kasuri|en|Kasuri|WD=Q3081545|lt=Kasuri|1=Kasuri}} kimono, wearing short pants, and holding bamboo cla11ers (sasara). These roles are played by boys aged 14–15, while all other roles are performed by adult men. * '''Saotome''' (早乙女): Several performers wearing Edo-style tsumugi kimono and {{ill|Okoso zukin|ja|おこそ頭巾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okoso zukin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} headdresses, carrying small fans and four-legged bamboo sticks.
The dance begins with the Kuwagashira's prologue and progresses through themes such as "Seed Planting," "Rice Planting," "Harvesting," "Threshing," "Hulling," "Using the Winnowing Machine," and "Ritual Completion tier 3]] {{Japan-culture-stub}}
}}

==Merged second translation== '''Tsushima no Taue Odori''' (津島の田植踊) is a {{ill|rice-planting dance|ja|田植踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice-planting dance|qq=}}, a form of folk performing art, passed down in {{ill|Tsunami, Namie|ja|浪江町津島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunami, Namie|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Namie, Fukushima|en|Namie, Fukushima|WD=Q1023937|lt=Namie, Fukushima|1=Namie, Fukushima}}, located in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|WD=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}, Japan. It has been designated as a {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation|lt=Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|選択無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 記録作成等の措置を講ずべき無形の民俗文化財}} by the national government and as a {{ill|Prefectural Cultural Property of Japan|ja|都道府県指定無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Cultural Property of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} by Fukushima Prefecture.
This rice-planting dance is a {{ill|Yoshuku|lt=Yoshuku|lt=Yoshuku-geino|ja|予祝芸能|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yoshuku-geino|qq=}} (ritual performance for blessings of bountiful harvests) originating from four {{ill|Human settlement|en|Human settlement|WD=Q486972|lt=settlements|1=Human settlement}} in Tsushima: Kamitsushima, Shimotsushima, Minamitsushima, and Akaogi. It was traditionally performed on the {{ill|14th day of the 1st lunar month|ja|1月14日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/14th day of the 1st lunar month|qq=}}, {{ill|15th day of the 1st lunar month|ja|1月15日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/15th day of the 1st lunar month|qq=}}, and {{ill|17th day of the 1st lunar month|ja|1月17日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/17th day of the 1st lunar month|qq=}}. Today, the dance is performed according to the Gregorian calendar. On the 14th, it is first offered at each settlement's {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}}, followed by performances at requested households.
The performers include: * '''Kuwakashira''' (鍬頭): Dressed in {{ill|naga-juban|ja|長襦袢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/naga-juban|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 襦袢|1=Zhongyi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55358825}} and tied with {{ill|donko|ja|緞子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/donko|1=Q11607976|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11607976}}, wearing a twisted {{ill|Tenugui|en|Tenugui|WD=Q2403941|lt=tenugui|1=Tenugui}} headband and carrying a large gourd. * '''Tane-oroshi''' (種下ろし): Two singers dressed in {{ill|formal kimono|ja|紋付羽織袴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/formal kimono|1=montsuki-haori-hakama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605315}}, armed with a {{ill|Wakizashi|en|Wakizashi|WD=Q221693|lt=wakizashi|1=Wakizashi}} and holding {{ill|Hand fan|en|Hand fan|WD=Q193220|lt=sensu|1=Hand fan}}. * '''Taiko-uchi''' (太鼓打ち): Two drummers wearing {{ill|formal hakama|ja|袷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/formal hakama|1=awase|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11626642}} tied with a {{ill|yaku-obi|ja|奴帯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yaku-obi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with rolled-up kimono hems, white {{ill|mohimo|ja|股引|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mohimo|1=momohiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611516}}, and {{ill|Kyahan|en|Kyahan|WD=Q6403679|lt=Kyahan|1=Kyahan}}, carrying {{ill|Drum|en|Drum|WD=Q11404|lt=taiko|1=Drum}} and sticks. * '''Sasara-zuri''' (ささら摺り): Two boys aged 14–15 wearing {{ill|Kasuri|en|Kasuri|WD=Q3081545|lt=kasuri|1=Kasuri}} garments, equipped with {{ill|Binzasara|en|Binzasara|WD=Q2904003|lt=Binzasara|1=Binzasara}} (wooden clappers). * '''Saotome''' (早乙女): Several adult male performers in {{ill|Edo-tsuma|ja|江戸褄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo-tsuma|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} kimono, wearing {{ill|Okoso zukin|ja|おこそ頭巾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okoso zukin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} hoods and holding {{ill|yotsutake|ja|四つ竹|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yotsutake|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or sensu.
The performance begins with a speech by the Kuwakashira, followed by dance sequences symbolizing "Seed Sowing," "Rice Planting," "Harvesting," "Threshing," "Milling," "Winnowing," and "Ending Ceremony." The ritual concludes with the Kuwakashira delivering congratulatory remarks.

hakama|ja|袷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/formal hakama|1=awase|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11626642}} tied with a {{ill|mohimo|ja|股引|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mohimo|1=momohiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611516}}, and {{ill|Kyahan|en|Kyahan|WD=Q6403679|lt=Kyahan|1=Kyahan}}, carrying {{ill|Drum|en|Drum|WD=Q11404|lt=taiko|1=Drum}} and sticks. * '''Sasara-zuri''' (ささら摺り): Two boys aged 14–15 wearing {{ill|Kasuri|en|Kasuri|WD=Q3081545|lt=kasuri|1=Kasuri}} garments, equipped with {{ill|Binzasara|en|Binzasara|WD=Q2904003|lt=Binzasara|1=Binzasara}} (wooden clappers). * '''Saotome''' (早乙女): Several adult male performers in {{ill|Edo-tsuma|ja|江戸褄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo-tsuma|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} kimono, wearing {{ill|Okoso zukin|ja|おこそ頭巾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okoso zukin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} hoods and holding {{ill|yotsutake|ja|四つ竹|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yotsutake|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or sensu.
The performance begins with a speech by the Kuwakashira, followed by dance sequences symbolizing "Seed Sowing," "Rice Planting," "Harvesting," "Threshing," "Milling," "Winnowing," and "Ending Ceremony." The ritual concludes with the Kuwakashira delivering congratulatory remarks.
== References == * "Matsuri, Geinō, Gyōji Daijiten (Vol. 2)" (朝倉書店, 2009), ISBN 978-4-254-50013-4.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:津島の田植踊]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:津島の田植踊]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:津島の田植踊]]
'''Tsushima no Taue Odori''' (津島の田植踊) is a traditional folk performance of rice planting dance from {{ill|Tsunami, Namie, Fukushima|ja|福島県双葉郡浪江町津島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunami, Namie, Fukushima|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Japan. It is recognized as a {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation|lt=Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|選択無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 記録作成等の措置を講ずべき無形の民俗文化財}} by the national government and as a prefectural {{ill|Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} of Fukushima Prefecture.
This rice planting dance is a form of {{ill|Yoshuku|lt=Yoshuku|lt=Yoshuku performing arts|ja|予祝芸能|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshuku performing arts|qq=}} that prays for abundant harvests, passed down through four villages in Namie: Kamitsushima, Shimotsushima, Minamitsushima, and Akugun. It was traditionally performed on {{ill|January 14th (lunar calendar)|ja|1月14日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 14th (lunar calendar)|1=January 14 (Lunisolar)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q839082}}, {{ill|January 15th (lunar calendar)|ja|1月15日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 15th (lunar calendar)|1=January 15 (Lunisolar)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q838395}}, and {{ill|January 17th (lunar calendar)|ja|1月17日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 17th (lunar calendar)|1=January 17 (Lunisolar)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q838983}} but is now conducted according to the Gregorian calendar. On the first day, January 14th, the dance is dedicated at each village's respective {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shrine|1=Shinto shrine}}, followed by performances at houses upon request.
The performance features distinct roles: * '''Kuwagashira''' (鍬頭): The leader who wears a {{ill|Nagajuban|ja|長襦袢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagajuban|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 襦袢|1=Zhongyi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55358825}}, secures a brocade sash (緞子), twists a {{ill|Tenugui|en|Tenugui|WD=Q2403941|lt=Tenugui|1=Tenugui}} around their head, and carries a large calabash. * '''Taneoroshi''' (種下ろし): Two performers in {{ill|Mon-tsuki haori hakama|ja|紋付羽織袴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mon-tsuki haori hakama|1=montsuki-haori-hakama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605315}}, equi11ed with short swords and folding fans, who sing. * '''Taiko-uchi''' (太鼓打ち): Two performers in mon-tsuki, tied with a {{ill|Yaku-himo|ja|奴帯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaku-himo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who wear white {{ill|Matabiki|ja|股引|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matabiki|1=momohiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611516}} and gaiters, playing drums with sticks. * '''Sasara-suri''' (ささら摺り): Two performers in {{ill|Kasuri|en|Kasuri|WD=Q3081545|lt=Kasuri|1=Kasuri}} kimono, wearing short pants, and holding bamboo cla11ers (sasara). These roles are played by boys aged 14–15, while all other roles are performed by adult men. * '''Saotome''' (早乙女): Several performers wearing Edo-style tsumugi kimono and {{ill|Okoso zukin|ja|おこそ頭巾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okoso zukin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} headdresses, carrying small fans and four-legged bamboo sticks.
The dance begins with the Kuwagashira's prologue and progresses through themes such as "Seed Planting," "Rice Planting," "Harvesting," "Threshing," "Hulling," "Using the Winnowing Machine," and "Ritual Completion tier 3]] {{Japan-culture-stub}}
}}

[[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] ==Merged second translation== '''Tsushima no Taue Odori''' (津島の田植踊) is a {{ill|rice-planting dance|ja|田植踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice-planting dance|qq=}}, a form of folk performing art, passed down in {{ill|Tsunami, Namie|ja|浪江町津島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunami, Namie|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Namie, Fukushima|en|Namie, Fukushima|WD=Q1023937|lt=Namie, Fukushima|1=Namie, Fukushima}}, located in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|WD=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}, Japan. It has been designated as a {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation|lt=Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|選択無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 記録作成等の措置を講ずべき無形の民俗文化財}} by the national government and as a {{ill|Prefectural Cultural Property of Japan|ja|都道府県指定無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Cultural Property of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} by Fukushima Prefecture.
This rice-planting dance is a {{ill|Yoshuku|lt=Yoshuku|lt=Yoshuku-geino|ja|予祝芸能|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yoshuku-geino|qq=}} (ritual performance for blessings of bountiful harvests) originating from four {{ill|Human settlement|en|Human settlement|WD=Q486972|lt=settlements|1=Human settlement}} in Tsushima: Kamitsushima, Shimotsushima, Minamitsushima, and Akaogi. It was traditionally performed on the {{ill|14th day of the 1st lunar month|ja|1月14日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/14th day of the 1st lunar month|qq=}}, {{ill|15th day of the 1st lunar month|ja|1月15日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/15th day of the 1st lunar month|qq=}}, and {{ill|17th day of the 1st lunar month|ja|1月17日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/17th day of the 1st lunar month|qq=}}. Today, the dance is performed according to the Gregorian calendar. On the 14th, it is first offered at each settlement's {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}}, followed by performances at requested households.
The performers include: * '''Kuwakashira''' (鍬頭): Dressed in {{ill|naga-juban|ja|長襦袢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/naga-juban|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 襦袢|1=Zhongyi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55358825}} and tied with {{ill|donko|ja|緞子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/donko|1=Q11607976|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11607976}}, wearing a twisted {{ill|Tenugui|en|Tenugui|WD=Q2403941|lt=tenugui|1=Tenugui}} headband and carrying a large gourd. * '''Tane-oroshi''' (種下ろし): Two singers dressed in {{ill|formal kimono|ja|紋付羽織袴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/formal kimono|1=montsuki-haori-hakama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605315}}, armed with a {{ill|Wakizashi|en|Wakizashi|WD=Q221693|lt=wakizashi|1=Wakizashi}} and holding {{ill|Hand fan|en|Hand fan|WD=Q193220|lt=sensu|1=Hand fan}}. * '''Taiko-uchi''' (太鼓打ち): Two drummers wearing {{ill|formal hakama|ja|袷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/formal hakama|1=awase|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11626642}} tied with a {{ill|yaku-obi|ja|奴帯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yaku-obi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with rolled-up kimono hems, white {{ill|mohimo|ja|股引|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mohimo|1=momohiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611516}}, and {{ill|Kyahan|en|Kyahan|WD=Q6403679|lt=Kyahan|1=Kyahan}}, carrying {{ill|Drum|en|Drum|WD=Q11404|lt=taiko|1=Drum}} and sticks. * '''Sasara-zuri''' (ささら摺り): Two boys aged 14–15 wearing {{ill|Kasuri|en|Kasuri|WD=Q3081545|lt=kasuri|1=Kasuri}} garments, equipped with {{ill|Binzasara|en|Binzasara|WD=Q2904003|lt=Binzasara|1=Binzasara}} (wooden clappers). * '''Saotome''' (早乙女): Several adult male performers in {{ill|Edo-tsuma|ja|江戸褄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo-tsuma|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} kimono, wearing {{ill|Okoso zukin|ja|おこそ頭巾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okoso zukin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} hoods and holding {{ill|yotsutake|ja|四つ竹|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yotsutake|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or sensu.
The performance begins with a speech by the Kuwakashira, followed by dance sequences symbolizing "Seed Sowing," "Rice Planting," "Harvesting," "Threshing," "Milling," "Winnowing," and "Ending Ceremony." The ritual concludes with the Kuwakashira delivering congratulatory remarks.
== References == * "Matsuri, Geinō, Gyōji Daijiten (Vol. 2)" (朝倉書店, 2009), ISBN 978-4-254-50013-4.

{{ill|Edo-tsuma|ja|江戸褄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo-tsuma|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} kimono, wearing {{ill|Okoso zukin|ja|おこそ頭巾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okoso zukin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} hoods and holding {{ill|yotsutake|ja|四つ竹|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yotsutake|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or sensu.
The performance begins with a speech by the Kuwakashira, followed by dance sequences symbolizing "Seed Sowing," "Rice Planting," "Harvesting," "Threshing," "Milling," "Winnowing," and "Ending Ceremony." The ritual concludes with the Kuwakashira delivering congratulatory remarks.
== References == * "Matsuri, Geinō, Gyōji Daijiten (Vol. 2)" (朝倉書店, 2009), ISBN 978-4-254-50013-4.

[[ja:津島の田植踊]] [[Category:Festivals in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Intangible Folk Cultural Properties that need measures such as documentation of Japan]] [[Category:Namie, Fukushima]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:Pre-celebration Performing Arts]] [[Category:Prefecturally designated intangible folk cultural property]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Winter Events in the Tohoku Region]] [[Category:pages]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:津島の田植踊]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:津島の田植踊]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:津島の田植踊]]
'''Tsushima no Taue Odori''' (津島の田植踊) is a traditional folk performance of rice planting dance from {{ill|Tsunami, Namie, Fukushima|ja|福島県双葉郡浪江町津島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunami, Namie, Fukushima|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Japan. It is recognized as a {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation|lt=Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|選択無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 記録作成等の措置を講ずべき無形の民俗文化財}} by the national government and as a prefectural {{ill|Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} of Fukushima Prefecture.
This rice planting dance is a form of {{ill|Yoshuku|lt=Yoshuku|lt=Yoshuku performing arts|ja|予祝芸能|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshuku performing arts|qq=}} that prays for abundant harvests, passed down through four villages in Namie: Kamitsushima, Shimotsushima, Minamitsushima, and Akugun. It was traditionally performed on {{ill|January 14th (lunar calendar)|ja|1月14日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 14th (lunar calendar)|1=January 14 (Lunisolar)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q839082}}, {{ill|January 15th (lunar calendar)|ja|1月15日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 15th (lunar calendar)|1=January 15 (Lunisolar)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q838395}}, and {{ill|January 17th (lunar calendar)|ja|1月17日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 17th (lunar calendar)|1=January 17 (Lunisolar)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q838983}} but is now conducted according to the Gregorian calendar. On the first day, January 14th, the dance is dedicated at each village's respective {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shrine|1=Shinto shrine}}, followed by performances at houses upon request.
The performance features distinct roles: * '''Kuwagashira''' (鍬頭): The leader who wears a {{ill|Nagajuban|ja|長襦袢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagajuban|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 襦袢|1=Zhongyi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55358825}}, secures a brocade sash (緞子), twists a {{ill|Tenugui|en|Tenugui|WD=Q2403941|lt=Tenugui|1=Tenugui}} around their head, and carries a large calabash. * '''Taneoroshi''' (種下ろし): Two performers in {{ill|Mon-tsuki haori hakama|ja|紋付羽織袴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mon-tsuki haori hakama|1=montsuki-haori-hakama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605315}}, equi11ed with short swords and folding fans, who sing. * '''Taiko-uchi''' (太鼓打ち): Two performers in mon-tsuki, tied with a {{ill|Yaku-himo|ja|奴帯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaku-himo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who wear white {{ill|Matabiki|ja|股引|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matabiki|1=momohiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611516}} and gaiters, playing drums with sticks. * '''Sasara-suri''' (ささら摺り): Two performers in {{ill|Kasuri|en|Kasuri|WD=Q3081545|lt=Kasuri|1=Kasuri}} kimono, wearing short pants, and holding bamboo cla11ers (sasara). These roles are played by boys aged 14–15, while all other roles are performed by adult men. * '''Saotome''' (早乙女): Several performers wearing Edo-style tsumugi kimono and {{ill|Okoso zukin|ja|おこそ頭巾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okoso zukin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} headdresses, carrying small fans and four-legged bamboo sticks.
The dance begins with the Kuwagashira's prologue and progresses through themes such as "Seed Planting," "Rice Planting," "Harvesting," "Threshing," "Hulling," "Using the Winnowing Machine," and "Ritual Completion tier 3]] {{Japan-culture-stub}}
}}

{{draft categories|
<!--Tsubame Taue Odori--><!--Tsubame Taue Odori--><!--Tsubame Taue Odori-->
[[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] ==Merged second translation== '''Tsushima no Taue Odori''' (津島の田植踊) is a {{ill|rice-planting dance|ja|田植踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice-planting dance|qq=}}, a form of folk performing art, passed down in {{ill|Tsunami, Namie|ja|浪江町津島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunami, Namie|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Namie, Fukushima|en|Namie, Fukushima|WD=Q1023937|lt=Namie, Fukushima|1=Namie, Fukushima}}, located in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|WD=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}, Japan. It has been designated as a {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation|lt=Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|選択無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 記録作成等の措置を講ずべき無形の民俗文化財}} by the national government and as a {{ill|Prefectural Cultural Property of Japan|ja|都道府県指定無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Cultural Property of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} by Fukushima Prefecture.
This rice-planting dance is a {{ill|Yoshuku|lt=Yoshuku|lt=Yoshuku-geino|ja|予祝芸能|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yoshuku-geino|qq=}} (ritual performance for blessings of bountiful harvests) originating from four {{ill|Human settlement|en|Human settlement|WD=Q486972|lt=settlements|1=Human settlement}} in Tsushima: Kamitsushima, Shimotsushima, Minamitsushima, and Akaogi. It was traditionally performed on the {{ill|14th day of the 1st lunar month|ja|1月14日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/14th day of the 1st lunar month|qq=}}, {{ill|15th day of the 1st lunar month|ja|1月15日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/15th day of the 1st lunar month|qq=}}, and {{ill|17th day of the 1st lunar month|ja|1月17日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/17th day of the 1st lunar month|qq=}}. Today, the dance is performed according to the Gregorian calendar. On the 14th, it is first offered at each settlement's {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}}, followed by performances at requested households.
The performers include: * '''Kuwakashira''' (鍬頭): Dressed in {{ill|naga-juban|ja|長襦袢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/naga-juban|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 襦袢|1=Zhongyi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55358825}} and tied with {{ill|donko|ja|緞子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/donko|1=Q11607976|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11607976}}, wearing a twisted {{ill|Tenugui|en|Tenugui|WD=Q2403941|lt=tenugui|1=Tenugui}} headband and carrying a large gourd. * '''Tane-oroshi''' (種下ろし): Two singers dressed in {{ill|formal kimono|ja|紋付羽織袴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/formal kimono|1=montsuki-haori-hakama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605315}}, armed with a {{ill|Wakizashi|en|Wakizashi|WD=Q221693|lt=wakizashi|1=Wakizashi}} and holding {{ill|Hand fan|en|Hand fan|WD=Q193220|lt=sensu|1=Hand fan}}. * '''Taiko-uchi''' (太鼓打ち): Two drummers wearing {{ill|formal hakama|ja|袷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/formal hakama|1=awase|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11626642}} tied with a {{ill|yaku-obi|ja|奴帯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yaku-obi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with rolled-up kimono hems, white {{ill|mohimo|ja|股引|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mohimo|1=momohiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611516}}, and {{ill|Kyahan|en|Kyahan|WD=Q6403679|lt=Kyahan|1=Kyahan}}, carrying {{ill|Drum|en|Drum|WD=Q11404|lt=taiko|1=Drum}} and sticks. * '''Sasara-zuri''' (ささら摺り): Two boys aged 14–15 wearing {{ill|Kasuri|en|Kasuri|WD=Q3081545|lt=kasuri|1=Kasuri}} garments, equipped with {{ill|Binzasara|en|Binzasara|WD=Q2904003|lt=Binzasara|1=Binzasara}} (wooden clappers). * '''Saotome''' (早乙女): Several adult male performers in {{ill|Edo-tsuma|ja|江戸褄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo-tsuma|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} kimono, wearing {{ill|Okoso zukin|ja|おこそ頭巾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okoso zukin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} hoods and holding {{ill|yotsutake|ja|四つ竹|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yotsutake|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or sensu.
The performance begins with a speech by the Kuwakashira, followed by dance sequences symbolizing "Seed Sowing," "Rice Planting," "Harvesting," "Threshing," "Milling," "Winnowing," and "Ending Ceremony." The ritual concludes with the Kuwakashira delivering congratulatory remarks.
== References == * "Matsuri, Geinō, Gyōji Daijiten (Vol. 2)" (朝倉書店, 2009), ISBN 978-4-254-50013-4.
